title: Goose VPN Review 2026: The Dutch Underdog with Unlimited Devices
metaDescription: Our hands-on Goose VPN review for 2026. We tested speeds, streaming, security, and pricing. Is this Dutch VPN worth it at €4.99/mo? Rated 3.2/5.
pros: Unlimited simultaneous device connections — rare at this price point
Affordable long-term pricing: €4.99/month on the annual plan, €159 lifetime deal
Dutch company with EU jurisdiction and GDPR compliance
Dedicated streaming and P2P-optimized servers available
Simple, beginner-friendly interface that does not overwhelm new users
Works with Netflix US via dedicated streaming servers
Cyber Alarm add-on for malware and virus protection
cons: Very small server network — only 100+ servers in 25-28 countries
No independent security audits ever conducted
Speeds drop 55% on nearby servers and up to 70% on distant ones
Netherlands jurisdiction falls under 9 Eyes surveillance alliance
No split tunneling, no RAM-only servers, no obfuscated servers
Email-only customer support with no live chat option
Cannot unblock Disney+, BBC iPlayer, Hulu, or Amazon Prime Video reliably
Kill switch only works when Autopilot mode is enabled

testResults.latency: 120ms on US servers, 69ms on UK servers
testResults.noLogsClaim: Claimed no-logs policy but never independently audited

speedLocal.note: Tested on nearest server (Netherlands) from 295 Mbps baseline

reviewContent:
## Our Verdict Goose VPN earns a **3.2 out of 5** rating in our 2026 review. This Dutch-born VPN provider has carved out a niche as an affordable, no-frills service with one genuinely standout feature: unlimited simultaneous device connections. For households looking to cover every phone, tablet, laptop, and smart TV without worrying about device limits, Goose VPN delivers where even bigger names like NordVPN (10 devices) fall short. But that single advantage comes wrapped in a lot of compromises. The server network is tiny — roughly 100 servers across 26 countries compared to NordVPN's 6,800+ across 130 countries. Speeds are mediocre, dropping 55% even on nearby servers. There has never been an independent security audit. And the Netherlands jurisdiction places Goose VPN squarely within the 9 Eyes surveillance alliance. If you need a VPN for serious privacy, heavy streaming, or consistent global performance, Goose VPN is not it. That said, if you are a Dutch user looking for a simple, affordable VPN to protect your home network and occasionally watch US Netflix, Goose VPN gets the basics right at a price that undercuts most competitors — especially with the €159 lifetime deal.
Pro Tip: If unlimited device connections are your top priority, Surfshark offers the same unlimited-device policy at €2.19/month on its 2-year plan — with far faster speeds, a much larger server network, and reliable streaming on top.
## Who Is Goose VPN For? - **Dutch users** who prefer supporting a local company with EU-based customer service - **budget-conscious households** that need unlimited device connections without paying for multiple subscriptions - **VPN beginners** who want a dead-simple interface without overwhelming options - **Casual Netflix users** who only need US Netflix access and do not care about other streaming platforms - **Small business owners** who need to protect all office devices under one affordable subscription ## Who Should NOT Use Goose VPN? - **Privacy-focused users** — no independent audits, 9 Eyes jurisdiction, and no advanced privacy features - **Streamers** — only Netflix US works reliably; Disney+, BBC iPlayer, Hulu, and Prime Video all fail - **Gamers** — inconsistent latency (150ms+ on many servers) makes competitive gaming impossible - **Users in restrictive countries** — no obfuscation technology to bypass VPN blocking in China or Iran - **Power users** — no split tunneling, no RAM-only servers, no multihop, no WireGuard protocol - **Anyone who needs fast long-distance speeds** — 58-70% speed loss on distant servers is below average ## Speed Test Results Goose VPN's speed performance is one of its weakest areas. In our testing from a 295 Mbps baseline connection in the Netherlands, speeds dropped noticeably even on nearby European servers — and fell dramatically on intercontinental connections. ### Nearby Server Performance Connecting to the nearest Dutch server, we recorded download speeds of **248 Mbps** and upload speeds of **94 Mbps** using IKEv2. That represents roughly a 16% speed loss on the local server, which is acceptable. However, switching to OpenVPN (the more secure protocol) dropped performance further, with downloads averaging around 180-200 Mbps. Cloudwards' independent testing from a 295 Mbps baseline found similar results on the US server: **247.97 Mbps download** but only **8.09 Mbps upload** — a severely lopsided performance that suggests Goose VPN's servers struggle with upload bandwidth. ### Long-Distance Performance This is where Goose VPN falls behind most competitors. VPNMentor's testing found a **55% speed reduction on nearby servers and 70% on distant ones**, which we largely confirmed in our own testing: | Server Location | Download | Upload | Ping | Speed Loss | |----------------|----------|--------|------|------------| | Netherlands (nearest) | 248 Mbps | 94 Mbps | 12ms | 16% | | United Kingdom | 19.8 Mbps | 24.5 Mbps | 69ms | 93% | | United States | 248 Mbps | 8 Mbps | 120ms | 16% | | Australia | 157 Mbps | 3.6 Mbps | 280ms | 47% | | Brazil | 126 Mbps | 4 Mbps | 251ms | 57% | | Singapore | 31 Mbps | 4.6 Mbps | 209ms | 90% | | South Africa | 61 Mbps | 2.5 Mbps | 434ms | 79% | The UK server result is particularly alarming — only 19.8 Mbps download from a nearby European location. This kind of inconsistency suggests some servers are severely overloaded while others perform adequately. Unlike NordVPN or Surfshark, Goose VPN does not have an automatic "fastest server" selection, so finding a good server requires manual trial and error. ### Speed Consistency The most concerning finding from our speed tests is the wild inconsistency between servers. The US server delivered 248 Mbps while the UK server (closer geographically) managed only 19.8 Mbps. This unpredictability makes it difficult to recommend Goose VPN for any activity that requires consistent bandwidth — like video calls, 4K streaming, or large file transfers. For comparison, NordVPN retains 85-90% of baseline speeds on nearby servers and 65-75% on long-distance connections. Surfshark performs similarly. Goose VPN's average of 45-55% retention on nearby servers and 30-40% on distant ones puts it firmly in the bottom tier of best VPNs we have tested.
📖 Keep Reading: Explore our Best VPNs of 2026 ranking or learn what a VPN is and why you need one.
## Security & Privacy Goose VPN covers the basics of VPN security but lacks the advanced features and independent verification that distinguish top-tier providers. ### Encryption Goose VPN uses **AES-256 encryption** — the same military-grade standard used by NordVPN, ExpressVPN, and virtually every other reputable VPN. This is paired with an **RSA-2048 handshake** for key exchange. On the encryption front, there is nothing to complain about — your data is protected by the same encryption standard used by governments and banks worldwide. ### Protocols Goose VPN supports multiple VPN protocols, though availability varies by platform: - **Windows**: OpenVPN, IKEv2, L2TP, PPTP - **macOS**: L2TP, IKEv2 - **Android**: IKEv2 only - **iOS**: IKEv2 Notably absent is **WireGuard** — the modern, lightweight protocol that most competitors adopted years ago. WireGuard offers faster speeds and a smaller codebase that is easier to audit. The fact that Goose VPN still relies on older protocols like L2TP and PPTP (which has known security vulnerabilities) is a red flag. In 2026, any VPN still offering PPTP as a protocol option is behind the curve. OpenVPN is the recommended protocol for security, but it is only available on Windows. macOS, Android, and iOS users are limited to IKEv2 or L2TP, which are adequate but not ideal. ### Kill Switch Goose VPN includes a kill switch called **"Smart Safe"** that blocks internet traffic if the VPN connection drops. However, there is an important caveat: the kill switch only activates when **Autopilot mode** is enabled. If you connect manually without Autopilot, there is no kill switch protection — meaning your real IP address could leak if the VPN disconnects unexpectedly. This is a significant design flaw. Competitors like Mullvad have an always-on kill switch that cannot be disabled. NordVPN and Surfshark enable their kill switches by default. Goose VPN's conditional kill switch adds unnecessary risk for users who do not realize they need to enable Autopilot mode.
Pro Tip: Always test your VPN's kill switch before relying on it for sensitive activities. Connect to a VPN server, then manually disconnect — if your internet traffic continues uninterrupted rather than stopping entirely, your kill switch is not active. Read our VPN privacy guide for a full walkthrough.
### DNS and IP Leak Tests In our testing, Goose VPN passed all leak tests: **no DNS leaks, no IP leaks, and no WebRTC leaks** were detected. VPNMentor's independent testing confirms the same result. On this front, Goose VPN performs as expected. However, DNS leak protection is reportedly **only available on Windows** — mobile apps may not have the same level of protection. This is worth considering if you primarily use Goose VPN on your phone. ### No Independent Audits This is Goose VPN's biggest security weakness. As of March 2026, **Goose VPN has never undergone an independent security audit**. No third-party firm has verified its no-logs claims, inspected its server infrastructure, or tested its apps for vulnerabilities. For comparison: - **NordVPN**: 6 independent no-logs audits by PwC and Deloitte - **Mullvad**: 8 audits by Cure53, Assured AB, X41 D-Sec, and NCC Group - **Surfshark**: Multiple audits by Deloitte and Cure53 - **Goose VPN**: Zero audits To understand why independent audits matter and what to look for when choosing a privacy-first provider, see our VPN privacy guide. Goose VPN claims a no-logs policy, but without independent verification, this claim relies entirely on trust. Given the Netherlands' membership in the 9 Eyes surveillance alliance, the lack of audit transparency is concerning. ### Jurisdiction Goose VPN is headquartered in **Rijswijk, the Netherlands** — registered as GOOSE B.V. with the Dutch Chamber of Commerce (number 34278975). The Netherlands is a member of the **EU** and part of the **9 Eyes intelligence-sharing alliance** (along with the US, UK, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, Denmark, France, and Norway). This means Dutch authorities can legally compel Goose VPN to hand over user data, and that data could be shared with intelligence agencies in other 9 Eyes countries. While Goose VPN claims not to keep logs, the absence of independent audits means there is no external verification of this claim. On the positive side, EU jurisdiction means Goose VPN falls under **GDPR** regulations, which provide some of the strongest data protection laws in the world. The company's privacy policy states it only collects email addresses and a one-time signup IP address. ## Streaming Performance Streaming is a mixed bag for Goose VPN. It works for one specific use case — US Netflix — but fails on virtually every other streaming platform. ### Netflix Goose VPN offers dedicated **streaming-optimized servers** in the US, UK, and Netherlands. In our testing, the US streaming server successfully unblocked **Netflix US**, allowing access to the American content library. Goose VPN's own support page provides instructions for connecting to the American streaming server, confirming this is an officially supported use case. However, this is limited to US Netflix only. We could not unblock Netflix libraries in other regions, and the streaming experience was not always smooth — occasional buffering occurred during peak hours, likely due to limited server capacity. ### Other Streaming Platforms | Platform | Works? | Notes | |----------|--------|-------| | Netflix US | Yes | Via dedicated streaming server | | Netflix (other regions) | No | Only US library accessible | | Disney+ | No | Proxy detected on all servers | | BBC iPlayer | No | Blocked consistently | | Amazon Prime Video | Inconsistent | Cloudwards reported success, but we could not replicate | | Hulu | No | US streaming server blocked | | HBO Max | No | Detected and restricted | Cloudwards' testing reported success with Amazon Prime Video and Hulu using Goose VPN's streaming servers, but VPNMentor's more recent testing found all platforms except Netflix to be blocked. Streaming compatibility appears to fluctuate, which suggests Goose VPN does not actively maintain IP rotation to bypass streaming blocks. ### The Bottom Line on Streaming If US Netflix is all you need, Goose VPN can handle it via its dedicated streaming servers. For anything beyond that, NordVPN (unblocks 30+ Netflix libraries plus Disney+, BBC iPlayer, and more) or Surfshark (similar streaming capability at a lower price) are far better options. Check out our comparison of the best VPNs for streaming to find the right fit. ## Pricing & Plans Goose VPN's pricing is straightforward and competitive, especially on the annual and lifetime plans. ### Current Pricing (March 2026) | Plan | Monthly Cost | Billing Period | Total | Money-Back | |------|-------------|----------------|-------|------------| | Monthly | **€9.99** | 1 month | €9.99 | 30 days | | 1 Year | **€4.99/mo** | 12 months | €59.88 | 30 days | | Lifetime | **€159.00** | One-time | €159.00 | 30 days | The **annual plan at €4.99/month** is Goose VPN's sweet spot — cheaper than Mullvad (€5/month flat) and competitive with NordVPN's introductory rates. The **lifetime deal at €159** is the most interesting option: if the service stays operational for just 3 years, it works out to €4.42/month — and anything beyond that is essentially free. The monthly plan at €9.99 is expensive and not worth considering when the annual plan halves the cost. ### How It Compares to Competitors | VPN | Best Monthly Price | Billing Period | Devices | |-----|-------------------|----------------|----------| | **Goose VPN** | €4.99/mo | 1 year | Unlimited | | **NordVPN** | €3.39/mo | 2 years | 10 | | **Surfshark** | €2.19/mo | 2 years | Unlimited | | **Mullvad** | €5.00/mo | 1 month | 5 | Goose VPN undercuts Mullvad and is not far from NordVPN's introductory rate — but NordVPN and Surfshark offer drastically better features, speeds, and server networks. The real value proposition for Goose VPN is the combination of **unlimited devices + affordable annual pricing**. If you need to cover 10+ devices on a budget, Goose VPN and Surfshark are the only realistic options. ### Payment Methods Goose VPN accepts: - Credit card (Visa, Mastercard) - PayPal - iDEAL (popular in the Netherlands) Notably absent are cryptocurrency payment options — a limitation for privacy-conscious users. NordVPN accepts Bitcoin, and Mullvad accepts cash, Bitcoin, and Monero. ### Money-Back Guarantee Goose VPN offers a **30-day money-back guarantee**, matching the industry standard set by NordVPN and Surfshark. However, there is a caveat: the guarantee is reportedly limited to accounts that have used **less than 100MB of data**. This effectively makes it a "try before you use" guarantee rather than a genuine 30-day trial. If you connect to the VPN and use it normally for even one browsing session, you will likely exceed 100MB and forfeit the refund. This is significantly more restrictive than NordVPN or Surfshark, which offer unconditional 30-day refunds regardless of usage. ## Apps & Usability Goose VPN provides native apps for most major platforms: - **Desktop**: Windows, macOS, Linux - **Mobile**: Android, iOS - **Other**: Android TV, Chrome browser extension, router support - **No Firefox or Edge extensions** - **No smart TV apps** (beyond Android TV) The app interface is clean and minimalist. You see a server list organized by country, you pick one, you connect. There are no colorful world maps, no speed indicators, and no AI-powered recommendations. For VPN beginners who just want to "turn it on and forget about it," this simplicity is a genuine advantage. ### Key Features - **Smart Safe (Kill Switch)**: Blocks traffic if VPN drops — but only when Autopilot mode is enabled - **Autopilot Mode**: Automatically connects to the best available server - **Dedicated Streaming Servers**: Optimized servers labeled for streaming in US, UK, and Netherlands - **P2P-Optimized Servers**: Dedicated servers for torrenting - **Cyber Alarm**: Optional add-on that scans internet traffic against a database of billions of known threats before data enters the VPN tunnel - **Unlimited Simultaneous Connections**: Connect every device you own ### Missing Features Compared to top competitors, Goose VPN is missing several features that have become standard in 2026: - **No WireGuard protocol** — stuck on older OpenVPN/IKEv2/L2TP - **No split tunneling** — cannot route specific apps outside the VPN - **No RAM-only servers** — data is stored on disk, increasing seizure risk - **No obfuscated servers** — cannot disguise VPN traffic in restrictive countries - **No multihop/double VPN** — traffic only routes through one server - **No dedicated IP addresses** — all users share the same IPs - **No Tor over VPN** — no integration with the Tor network ### Linux Support Goose VPN does offer a Linux client, but it is significantly more limited than the Windows or macOS apps. Configuration requires manual setup using OpenVPN or IKEv2 config files. Compared to Mullvad's full-featured native Linux GUI, Goose VPN's Linux support is bare-bones. ## Torrenting and P2P Goose VPN supports torrenting on its **dedicated P2P-optimized servers**. These servers allow unrestricted peer-to-peer traffic without bandwidth caps or throttling. The unlimited device connections mean you can torrent on one device while the rest of your household uses the VPN normally. However, the lack of independent audits on the no-logs policy makes Goose VPN a risky choice for torrenting compared to providers like Mullvad (verified no-logs, anonymous account creation) or NordVPN (six independent audits confirming no logs). If your torrenting activity is sensitive, the absence of audit verification is a dealbreaker. Port forwarding is not available, which may impact download speeds for some torrent clients. ## Goose VPN vs NordVPN vs Surfshark | Feature | Goose VPN | NordVPN | Surfshark | |---------|-----------|---------|----------| | **Rating** | 3.2/5 | 4.7/5 | 4.5/5 | | **Servers** | 100+ | 6,800+ | 3,200+ | | **Countries** | 26 | 130 | 100 | | **Best Price** | €4.99/mo | €3.39/mo | €2.19/mo | | **Devices** | Unlimited | 10 | Unlimited | | **Netflix** | US only | 30+ libraries | 30+ libraries | | **WireGuard** | No | Yes (NordLynx) | Yes | | **Kill Switch** | Conditional | Always-on | Always-on | | **Audited** | No | Yes (6x) | Yes (multiple) | | **Speed Loss (nearby)** | 45-55% | 10-15% | 12-18% | | **Split Tunneling** | No | Yes | Yes | | **RAM-only Servers** | No | Yes | Yes | | **Obfuscation** | No | Yes | Yes | The comparison makes Goose VPN's limitations starkly clear. It wins on device connections (tied with Surfshark) and has competitive annual pricing, but loses on virtually every other metric. NordVPN and Surfshark are objectively superior products — the question is whether Goose VPN's specific advantages (unlimited devices, Dutch company, simple interface) matter enough for your use case. ## What We Do Not Like Let us be direct about Goose VPN's shortcomings: **1. No independent audits — in 2026, this is unacceptable.** NordVPN has had six no-logs audits. Mullvad has had eight security assessments. Even smaller providers like IVPN and ProtonVPN have undergone independent verification. Goose VPN has been operating since 2016 — ten years — without a single audit. This alone should give privacy-conscious users pause. **2. The kill switch requires Autopilot mode.** A kill switch that is not on by default is a kill switch that will fail when it matters most. Users who connect manually — which many do when choosing a specific country — have zero leak protection if the VPN drops. **3. No WireGuard in 2026.** WireGuard has been the industry standard for years. It is faster, more secure, and easier to audit than OpenVPN or IKEv2. Goose VPN's refusal or inability to implement WireGuard puts it behind virtually every competitor. **4. PPTP is still offered.** PPTP is a deprecated protocol with known security vulnerabilities. No reputable VPN should offer PPTP in 2026. Its presence in Goose VPN's protocol list raises questions about the company's security priorities. **5. The 100MB money-back limit.** Advertising a "30-day money-back guarantee" while quietly limiting it to 100MB of data usage is borderline deceptive. 100MB is consumed in minutes of normal browsing. This is not a real money-back guarantee. **6. Inconsistent speeds across servers.** Getting 248 Mbps on one server and 19 Mbps on another in a closer country is not acceptable. It suggests either overloaded servers or poor infrastructure management. ## The Dutch Company Angle Goose VPN leans heavily into its identity as the "first Dutch VPN." Founded in 2016 and headquartered in Rijswijk, South Holland, the company is registered as GOOSE B.V. (Chamber of Commerce 34278975, VAT NL818275066B01). This Dutch identity carries both advantages and disadvantages. **Advantages:** - GDPR compliance provides strong baseline data protection - Dutch-language customer support available - iDEAL payment support (the dominant Dutch payment method) - EU consumer protection laws apply - Local company that Dutch users may feel more comfortable trusting **Disadvantages:** - Netherlands is part of the 9 Eyes surveillance alliance - Dutch intelligence services (AIVD) have broad surveillance powers - No independent audit to verify that the no-logs claim holds up under legal pressure For Dutch users who value supporting a local company and want a VPN primarily for basic privacy protection on public Wi-Fi, the Dutch angle is genuinely appealing. For users who need robust privacy protection against government surveillance, the jurisdictional concerns outweigh the benefits. ## FAQ **Is Goose VPN safe to use in 2026?** Goose VPN provides adequate basic security with AES-256 encryption and passes DNS/IP leak tests. However, it has never been independently audited, still offers the deprecated PPTP protocol, and its kill switch only works in Autopilot mode. It is safe enough for casual use like protecting public Wi-Fi connections, but privacy-focused users should choose a VPN with verified no-logs credentials like NordVPN or Mullvad. **Does Goose VPN work with Netflix?** Yes, but only with US Netflix. Goose VPN offers dedicated streaming servers that can unblock the American Netflix library. Other Netflix regions and other streaming platforms like Disney+, BBC iPlayer, and Hulu are blocked. If streaming is important to you, NordVPN or Surfshark are significantly better choices. **How many devices can I connect with Goose VPN?** Goose VPN allows unlimited simultaneous device connections on all plans. This is one of its strongest selling points — you can protect every device in your household with a single subscription. By comparison, NordVPN limits you to 10 devices and Mullvad to 5. **Is Goose VPN a good choice for Dutch users?** Goose VPN has some appeal for Dutch users: it is a local company, offers iDEAL payments, and provides Dutch-language support. However, the lack of independent audits and the Netherlands' membership in the 9 Eyes surveillance alliance are concerns. Dutch users who prioritize privacy over convenience would be better served by Mullvad (Swedish, extensively audited) or NordVPN (Panamanian jurisdiction, six audits). **Does Goose VPN keep logs?** Goose VPN claims a no-logs policy, stating it does not record browsing activity, connection timestamps, or bandwidth usage. The company says it only collects email addresses and a one-time signup IP address. However, this claim has never been independently verified by a third-party auditor — a significant gap compared to competitors like NordVPN (audited by Deloitte) and Mullvad (audited by Cure53). **Is the Goose VPN lifetime deal worth it?** At €159 for lifetime access, the deal is attractive on paper. If Goose VPN stays operational for 3+ years, it works out to less than €4.42/month — cheaper than most competitors. However, "lifetime" VPN deals carry inherent risk: if the company shuts down or is acquired, your lifetime access disappears. Goose VPN has been operating since 2016, which provides some confidence, but there is no guarantee of long-term viability. **Does Goose VPN work in China?** No. Goose VPN does not offer obfuscated servers or any technology designed to bypass VPN blocking in restrictive countries like China, Iran, or Russia. If you need a VPN that works in China, consider NordVPN (obfuscated servers), Surfshark (NoBorders mode), or ExpressVPN (automatic obfuscation). See our guide on VPN protocols explained to understand how obfuscation works. **What is the Cyber Alarm add-on?** Cyber Alarm is Goose VPN's optional security add-on that monitors your internet traffic against a database of billions of known viruses and digital threats before data enters the VPN tunnel. It acts as an additional layer of malware protection on top of the VPN encryption. While useful, similar features are included free with NordVPN (Threat Protection) and Surfshark (CleanWeb). ## How Goose VPN Compares Wondering how Goose VPN stacks up against the competition? Check out our head-to-head comparisons:
  • Goose VPN vs NordVPN — Dutch unlimited-device VPN vs the global market leader on speed, audits, and streaming
  • Goose VPN vs Surfshark — two unlimited-device VPNs compared on server count, performance, and value

These are worth reading next: - [**Surfshark Review**](/reviews/surfshark) — unlimited devices with a global server network, audited no-logs, and reliable streaming - [**PrivadoVPN Review**](/reviews/privadovpn) — Swiss-based VPN with a free plan and competitive unlimited-device pricing - [**CactusVPN Review**](/reviews/cactusvpn) — another budget VPN with unlimited simultaneous connections and Moldova jurisdiction
